metadata.task: eRisk 2026 Task 2 — Contextualized Early Detection of Depression

task2_field_size.note: spec assumed 'field of 63' — actual field is 70 runs from 17 teams (see Table 5)

local_vs_official_consistency.alert_counts: Local exactly reproduces official decision counts (R0 total = 101 alerts; TP counts = round(R * 91); FP = total - TP).
local_vs_official_consistency.median_tp_latency: Local medians are -2 rounds relative to official (R0 local 11 vs official 13; R1 local 7 vs official 9). Difference is a constant round-indexing offset (local file rounds are 0-indexed; official counts writings 1-indexed, plus possibly the initial context post).

G1_1_ranking_metrics_per_run_at_500_writ.source: PDF Table 7 — INSA-Lyon rows at 500 writings
G1_1_ranking_metrics_per_run_at_500_writ.notes: Trajectory: R0/R1/R3/R4 reach 1.00 P@10 and NDCG@10 by 100 writings and stay there; NDCG@100 rises from 0.64 (1 writing) to 0.84 (100w), 0.87 (250w), 0.89 (500w). R2 trails by ~0.04 on NDCG@100 throughout.

G1_2_field_rank_and_leader_values.source: PDF Tables 6 and 7 — INSA-Lyon position within full field of 70 runs

F1.spec_assumption_correction: spec said 'best of our portfolio = 0.76' — actual best is 0.80 (R0 and R4 both at 0.80; R3 at 0.75 was probably the value remembered)

NDCG_at_100.rank_in_field: tied at 3 of 70 (nine runs share 0.89: 5 NoirByte runs + 4 INSA-Lyon runs)
NDCG_at_100.spec_assumption_correction: spec said 'NDCG@100 ... 0.87 (already known)' — actual field max at 500 writings is 0.91 (DeepCare run 0). Our best is 0.89, not the field max.

ERDE_5.note: Lower is better for ERDE. HUTECH-NLP runs 0 and 3 both reach 0.08–0.09. R1 (0.15) is mid-pack.

G2_1_per_run_alert_counts.verification: Local TP counts and totals exactly match what would be derived from Table 6's P and R columns (e.g., R0: TP = R*91 = 77, total = TP/P = 77/0.76 = 101). The 101-alert figure for R0 mentioned in the §5.1 ¶2 draft is confirmed.
G2_1_per_run_alert_counts.phase_split_for_R0: R0 first_alert distribution: 50 alerts fired in rounds ≤ 50 (Phase 1), 51 fired in rounds 51–499 (Phase 2). The original '50 in Phase 1' draft sentence is consistent with the local data.

G2_2_per_run_median_tp_latency.offset: Local values are exactly 2 rounds below official across all 5 runs — consistent round-indexing offset (local rounds 0-indexed; official 1-indexed plus initial context).
G2_2_per_run_median_tp_latency.recommended_for_paper: Use the official Table 6 values (13, 9, 7.5, 11.5, 14) since the paper references official Table 5 / Table 6 numbers throughout.

rows.note: TP. Larger-gap example. Mentioned in §1.1 illustrative example (sparse target text + community concern).
TP. Sits at the median of the 28-subject early-alert cohort (median gap = 21).
Control (gold = 0). R0 never alerts (true negative). R1 false-alerts at round 7 (false positive). Spec marked this as 'n/a (control)' for the gap column.
TP. Among the very-large-gap subjects in the cohort (gap = 95).

early_alert_cohort_summary.definition: TPs (gold = 1) where both R0 and R1 alert AND R1 alerts ≥ 5 rounds before R0

G2_4_per_run_training_mean_alert_round.status: PARTIAL — local mean is reportable (over the 522-subject test cohort), but the 158 / 114 numbers from the prior draft cannot be reproduced
G2_4_per_run_training_mean_alert_round.explanation: The 'training-simulation mean alert round' values 158 (R0) and 114 (R1) cited in the prior draft must come from a separate training-simulation log (likely the 2025 train data with 909 users, processed across longer round sequences) which is not present in the local runs/task2/ directory. The available decision files (runs/task2/train/decisions/) correspond to the 522 2026 test subjects (matching the 2026 gold truth file).
G2_4_per_run_training_mean_alert_round.recommendation: Either drop the train/test comparison from §5.4 ¶2, or re-run the system on the 2025 training corpus (909 users) to regenerate the training-mean values. Local re-scoring on test data does not substitute for it.
